title: Acacia neobrachycarpa
taxonomy: Acacia neobrachycarpa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Acacia, which includes over 1,300 species of trees, shrubs, and climbers. Acacia neobrachycarpa is native to Australia, where it can be found growing in dry woodlands and grasslands. The species is characterized by its small, narrow leaves and its ability to fix nitrogen in the soil through symbiotic relationships with soil bacteria. Acacia neobrachycarpa is also known to produce small, yellow flowers that are an important food source for various pollinators and other insects.
